entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: A pulse crop grown in semi-arid tropics that is susceptible to Fusarium wilt

entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: A pulse crop grown in semi-arid tropics that is susceptible to Fusarium wilt

entities.name: LENTIL
entities.description: A pulse crop grown in semi-arid tropics that is susceptible to Fusarium wilt

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: Regions characterized by low rainfall and high temperatures where pulse crops are major components of agriculture

entities.name: MALNUTRITION
entities.description: A health condition alleviated by the dietary proteins provided by pulses

entities.name: FUSARIUM WILT
entities.description: A devastating disease that causes significant yield losses in pulse crops

entities.name: FUSARIUM
entities.description: The pathogen responsible for causing Fusarium wilt

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The productivity of food legumes, which can be severely constrained by disease

entities.name: WILT RESISTANCE
entities.description: The genetic ability of a crop to resist Fusarium wilt

entities.name: SCREENING TECHNIQUES
entities.description: Methods used to identify genotypes resistant to Fusarium wilt

entities.name: GENETIC DISSECTION
entities.description: The process of analyzing the genetic components of a trait

entities.name: MAPPING
entities.description: The process of identifying the chromosomal location of resistance genes

entities.name: TAGGING
entities.description: The process of associating molecular markers with specific resistance genes

entities.name: MOLECULAR MARKERS
entities.description: DNA sequences used in the tagging and mapping of resistance genes

entities.name: GENE PYRAMIDING
entities.description: The strategy of combining multiple resistance genes into a single variety to resist multiple pathogen races

entities.name: YIELD GAP
entities.description: The difference between the potential yield and the realized yield of a crop
